metadata.raw_text: {"scores": {"clarity": 5, "simplicity": 5, "brevity_economy": 5, "structure_flow": 5, "specificity_precision": 5, "humanity_voice": 4, "overall": 5}, "rationale": "Exceptionally well-crafted memo. The three-section structure (what happened, what we fixed, what to expect) is immediately scannable and logical. Specific timestamps (9:12 AM, 9:45 AM, 12:08 PM), concrete technical details (load balancer config, authentication service, session tokens), and precise next steps (Thursday 2 PM post-mortem, Friday EOD report, new approval process) make this highly informative without being bloated. Language is plain and direct throughout—no jargon inflation or corporate filler. The closing paragraph acknowledges employee frustration and thanks specific behaviors (switching to offline workflows), which adds genuine warmth. The only slight knock on humanity/voice is that it reads as a very polished template—almost too perfectly structured—which makes it feel slightly less spontaneous than a real human voice, though this is a minor quibble for a formal internal memo where polish is appropriate."}

rationale: Exceptionally well-crafted memo. The three-section structure (what happened, what we fixed, what to expect) is immediately scannable and logical. Specific timestamps (9:12 AM, 9:45 AM, 12:08 PM), concrete technical details (load balancer config, authentication service, session tokens), and precise next steps (Thursday 2 PM post-mortem, Friday EOD report, new approval process) make this highly informative without being bloated. Language is plain and direct throughout—no jargon inflation or corporate filler. The closing paragraph acknowledges employee frustration and thanks specific behaviors (switching to offline workflows), which adds genuine warmth. The only slight knock on humanity/voice is that it reads as a very polished template—almost too perfectly structured—which makes it feel slightly less spontaneous than a real human voice, though this is a minor quibble for a formal internal memo where polish is appropriate.
